_: SME × Lark landing page copy. Single source of truth for the /sme/ page. Edit this file, run `python3 build/build.py`, and sme/index.html regenerates. Section structure mirrors templates/sme/*.html — keep keys aligned with placeholders. SEO metadata (title / description / canonical) lives separately in content/seo.json under [smePage].

hero.eyebrow: For the SMEs the mainstream forgot
hero.h1Line1: The same carrier-grade stack the big banks use —
hero.tagline: SMS, WhatsApp, Voice, Email — and your ERP — all flowing through your Lark workspace. The big CPaaS players (Twilio / Vonage / MessageBird) won't return your call below USD $50k annual contract. We will. Pay per message from SGD $0.04, native Lark integration, multi-language by default. Live tonight, no IT team needed.

mockupMessages.text: Hi! I'd like to confirm tomorrow's 3pm appointment.
Confirmed for 3pm! See you tomorrow at Robinson Rd.

benefits.eyebrow: Why SMEs choose us over Twilio + Slack
benefits.title: Three things the mainstream won't give you.
benefits.intro: The big CPaaS vendors built their pricing for VC-funded scale-ups in San Francisco. We built ours for a 12-person clinic in District 5, a 40-person logistics firm in Penang, a family bakery chain in Tsim Sha Tsui. Different game, different rules.

items.title: Lives inside Lark.
items.desc: No new dashboard for your team to learn. SMS / WhatsApp / Voice / Email all flow into a single Lark group chat where your existing team already lives. Reply from Lark, the customer gets it on their channel of choice.

items.title: Pay per message. No floor.
items.desc: No US$ 1,000 / month enterprise minimum. No annual commit. From SGD $0.04 per SMS, WhatsApp from $0.03, voice minutes by the second. Top up SGD $20 today, send 500 messages, pause for a month — fine.

items.title: Asian-language native, end to end.
items.desc: Built in Singapore, run across ASEAN. SMS in 繁體 / 简体 / Tiếng Việt / ไทย / Bahasa / Tagalog deliver as native scripts, not transliterated nonsense. Templates pre-localised. Support replies in your language, not Google-translated.
bullets: Unicode SMS in all CJK + SEA scripts
Support team in SG, KL, HK, BKK, HCM

items.title: Compliance, automatic.
items.desc: PDPA Singapore / Malaysia / Thailand · 个保法 · PDPL Vietnam · PDPO Hong Kong · GDPR — every regulation gets checked at the routing layer before the message leaves. No legal team required. Quarterly audit reports auto-generated; you forward them to your auditor and move on.
bullets: Sender ID / opt-out / DND windows enforced per region
Tamper-evident message logs (same format the banks file)
ISO 27001 + SOC 2 inherited from enterprise stack

pricing.title: App, Cloud, or Platform. Same backbone.
pricing.intro: Same carrier-grade backbone — different ways of delivering it to you. App for fast self-serve, Cloud for owned dashboards and webhooks, Platform for private deployment and white-label. Move up between modes when you grow; your message history, Sender IDs, and Lark integration carry over intact.
pricing.fineprint: Per-region outgoing rates on the enterprise page. Receiving SMS & voice into Lark is always free. SGD prices include GST; we also bill in HKD / VND / THB / IDR / MYR / PHP where supported.

plans.name: App
plans.tagline: Lark App Center · 1 – 50 person teams · 5-min setup

features: SMS + WhatsApp + Voice + Email + ERP webhooks
Own dashboard · own Sender ID · own webhooks
Data residency: SG / HK / VN / TH (you pick)

plans.tagline: Private deployment · 500+ teams or regulated industries
features: Private deployment in your VPC / on-prem / sovereign cloud
White-label option · custom SLA · custom routing
All channels + RCS + WhatsApp Business + custom

useCases.title: Built for the un-glamorous, mission-critical work.
useCases.intro: Six common shapes of SME we serve. Yours probably looks a lot like one of these — your implementation will land in 10 minutes via the same Lark integration.

items.title: Recover abandoned carts in the same conversation thread.
items.desc: Shopify / Shopee / Lazada cart abandonment fires an SMS or WhatsApp template, customer replies, your team picks it up in Lark — same thread, no app-switching.

items.title: Booking confirmations that cut no-shows from 18% to 7%.
items.desc: Lark Calendar event triggers a 24-hr SMS reminder. One-tap reply confirms, cancels, or reschedules. Your floor manager sees it in Lark before service starts. Field data from a 12-store HK chain over Q4 2025.

items.title: Appointment reminders patients actually read.
items.desc: 98% verified open rate on transactional SMS — same carrier-grade routes the banks use. Reschedule requests land in Lark for your front desk to triage in seconds.

items.title: Order updates in the customer's language, automatically.
items.desc: Shipped, customs-cleared, out-for-delivery, delivered — all four updates routed to the right channel (SMS for SG/MY, WhatsApp for ID/PH, Email for VN), localised on send.

items.title: Parent–teacher comms in one inbox.
items.desc: Bulk announcements via SMS, individual replies via WhatsApp, teachers respond from their Lark workspace. PDPA-compliant by default, parents stay in the channel they prefer.

items.title: Driver dispatch in Lark, customer tracking via SMS.
items.desc: Lark Approval routes the job to a driver, the driver accepts in Lark, the customer gets an SMS with live ETA — all from one workflow, no third-party fleet app required.

integrations.title: Where your ERP, your team, and your customer meet.
integrations.intro: Most SMEs aren't asking 'how do I send an SMS' — they're asking 'how do I get an SAP order to trigger a customer SMS, with my team seeing it in Lark, with the audit trail in one place'. Three workflows cover ~90% of what real SMEs need. Mix and match.

flows.title: ERP order → Lark group notification
flows.desc: New order, low-stock alert, refund request, KPI breach — your ERP fires a webhook to DOLPHLINK, we route it through the right template and post to the right Lark group. End-to-end latency under 3 seconds. Each message carries an 'open in ERP' deep link.

nodes.label: ERP
nodes.sub: SAP / 用友 / 金蝶 / Odoo

flows.title: Lark Approval → DOLPHLINK customer notification
flows.desc: Customer submits a quote / refund / address-change request. Lark Approval handles internal sign-off (with the audit trail your auditor wants). On approval, DOLPHLINK reaches the customer on their preferred channel. Both sides bound to the same Lark thread for traceability.

flows.title: Lark Calendar → automatic customer reminder
flows.desc: Bookings, renewal calls, on-site service, contract expiry — Lark Calendar event fires reminders 24h / 2h / 30min before. Multi-language, channel-routed, and customer replies (YES / RESCHEDULE) flow back into the same Lark thread. F&B field data: no-show rate dropped from 18% to 7%.

howItWorks.title: Three steps. Live before lunch.
howItWorks.intro: Most SME onboardings finish in under 10 minutes. The slowest step is waiting for the SMS verification code — usually 30 seconds, sometimes 90 if your carrier is slow.

steps.title: Connect Lark.
steps.desc: Open the DOLPHLINK app inside Lark App Center, click Authorise. We never read your existing Lark messages — we only post into a new group chat we create on first run.

steps.title: Verify your business number.
steps.desc: Enter the mobile number customers should see as the sender, click Verify. We send a one-time SMS code, you reply 6 digits in Lark. Done.

steps.title: Send your first message.
steps.desc: Pick a template (we ship 20+ pre-localised for common SME flows), or type your own. Send to one number, see the Lark thread populate with delivery + read receipts.

cta.title: The mainstream is built for the top 1%. We built this for the other 99%.
cta.desc: If you're a 12-person clinic, a 30-store F&B chain, a cross-border logistics outfit, a tuition centre, or any SME the big CPaaS sales teams won't return your calls — this is for you. Connect Lark, send your first message tonight, talk to a human if you need help.

footer.tag: Carrier-grade communications for the SMEs the mainstream forgot. By Baiwu Technology Group, Singapore.
